Mind the Waste
01 / 10

Mind the Waste

A fridge sensor that eliminates food waste with AI.

The problem

UK households waste £14 billion of food a year

£470
binned per UK household a year
£14bn
of food wasted across UK homes a year
6.7Mt
UK household food waste, most of it avoidable
We go after the avoidable half: catch food before it turns, and put the money straight back in people's pockets.
The product

A smart fridge magnet

It sticks to the fridge door, photographs the shelves, tracks temperature and humidity, and sends what it finds to your phone.

CAM

5MP camera

ENV

Temp + humidity

AI

On-device AI

How it works

Camera and climate sensors predict when food will spoil

1 · See

A vision model names each item and grades freshness: fresh, use soon, spoiling.

→

2 · Sense

Temp and humidity log the microclimate. A warm, damp fridge spoils food faster.

→

3 · Predict

Sight and climate fuse into days-to-spoil, then a nudge: cook, freeze, share.

+12%
accuracy gain from fusing camera and gas over a single sensor
94%
freshness classification with camera plus gas in published trials
±0.2°C
temperature accuracy of the on-board sensor (RH ±2%)
System architecture

How the device talks to the AI

Business model

Four revenue streams

Subscriptions
  • Basic free
  • Standard £4.99/mo
  • Pro £9.99/mo
Hardware
  • £79.99 retail
  • £25.15 unit cost
  • ~68% gross margin
Partnerships
  • Commission on sales
  • Grocery + delivery
  • +30% users a year
Advertising
  • Native ads
  • Reward ads
  • Brand placements
Market size

TAM£1.75T
SAM£98.75B

Possible extensions

Where Mind the Waste goes next

Gas sensing

A gas sensor smells spoilage before it is visible, and works on packaged food the camera cannot see inside.

Health link

Connect the fridge to your glucose and mood, then suggest meals from what is inside that keep you steady.

Auto-restock

One tap to reorder what is running low, sent straight to a grocery-delivery basket.

Beyond the home

The same sensor for cafes, canteens and retailers to cut food waste at scale.
